content.problemStatement:
Rectify the errors in the program to get the desired output.

Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 10). Each of the next T lines contains a single integer choice.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line. If choice is 1, 2 or 3 print "Option X selected" where X is the choice. For any other value print "Invalid choice".

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1 and the only choice is 1, so the program prints the corresponding message.
T=1 and the only choice is 2, so the program prints the corresponding message.
T=1 and the only choice is 3, so the program prints the corresponding message.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected
Option 1 selected

hidden.label: maximum boundary 3
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected

hidden.label: uniform case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 2 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 2 selected

hidden.label: structured case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected

hidden.label: structured case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 3 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 1 selected

hidden.label: tricky case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected

hidden.label: stress case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected

solution.approach: The problem only requires a direct mapping from the input integer to a fixed output string. For each test case we read the integer `choice`. If it equals 1, 2 or 3 we output the corresponding message; otherwise we output "Invalid choice". This is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used besides the output buffer.
